methods.description: Statistical analysis of species diversity, richness, and evenness to measure community structure and compare means among different substrates, sampling seasons, and site categories.

steps.description: Species diversity (H'), richness (S) and evenness (E) were calculated following Shannon (1949).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate species diversity, richness, and evenness

steps.description: A nonparametric test, Kruskal-Wallis, with Mann-Whitney pair-wise comparisons and Bonferroni correlation, was used to compare means of S, H´ and E among substrates sampled, and among the 4 sampling seasons and 3 sampling site categories.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Kruskal-Wallis test with pair-wise comparisons

methods.description: Analysis of environmental variables using transformations and two-way ANOVA to compare means among sampling periods and site categories.

steps.description: The distributions of turbidity, conductivity, TDS, width, and embeddedness were positively skewed, and therefore were ln( × + 1) transformed.
steps.searchable_summary: apply log transformation to environmental variables

steps.description: Two-way analysis of variance (two-way ANOVA) was used to compare means of environmental variables among the 4 sampling periods and among the 3 site categories.
steps.searchable_summary: perform two-way ANOVA on environmental variables

methods.description: Multivariate analysis of diatom data to identify floristic variation gradients and relationships with environmental variables using DCA and CCA.

steps.description: Preliminary de-trended correspondence analysis (DCA) was applied to the diatom data set to determine the length of the gradient.
Canonical Correspondence Analysis (CCA) was used to investigate relationships between predictor variables and benthic diatom communities.
steps.searchable_summary: perform CCA to analyze diatom community relationships

steps.description: Monte Carlo permutation tests (999 unrestricted permutations, p ≤ 0.05) were used to test the significance of the axis.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct Monte Carlo permutation tests for axis significance

methods.description: Collection and preparation of diatom samples from various substrates for laboratory analysis.

steps.description: Epilithic diatoms were sampled by scraping stones with a toothbrush.
steps.searchable_summary: collect epilithic diatoms by scraping stones

steps.description: Epiphytic diatoms were sampled after Fisher and Dunbar (2007).
steps.searchable_summary: collect epiphytic diatoms following Fisher and Dunbar method

steps.description: Epipelic and epipsammic diatoms were sampled by pressing a Petri dish lid into the top layer of sand or silt/clay.
steps.searchable_summary: collect epipelic and epipsammic diatoms using Petri dish

steps.description: Sub-samples of the diatom suspensions were cleaned of organic material using wet combustion with concentrated sulphuric acid.
steps.searchable_summary: clean diatom samples using wet combustion

steps.description: Three replicate slides were prepared for each sample.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare replicate slides for diatom samples

steps.description: Up to 600 valves per sample were identified and counted using the phase contrast light microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: identify and count diatom valves using microscope
